stories: Good evening Sean, it's Monday evening, the twenty-fifth of May, 2026
Fun fact to start the evening: today is Geek Pride Day — chosen because May twenty-fifth marks the anniversary of the original Star Wars release in 1977
The galaxy far, far away is forty-nine years old and still going strong
Looking ahead to tomorrow — you have Big Rock Time from two in the afternoon until half past five, a solid deep-work block
Then late in the evening, Safe Start at eleven on Webex for thirty minutes — the cross-timezone check-in with the Canadian team
On the Singapore front today, some encouraging economic data
April inflation came in lower than expected at one point eight percent, and first-quarter GDP was revised sharply upward to six percent, from an earlier estimate of four point six
The Monetary Authority expects full-year inflation to stay between one point five and two point five percent
Good morning Sean, it's Monday, the twenty-fifth of May
Fun fact to start the week: today is Africa Day — marking the founding of the African Union in 1963
And as you'll hear, the politics around this year's celebrations are rather charged
Worth knowing too: May is the only month in the English calendar that cannot be abbreviated
January becomes Jan, February becomes Feb — but May simply refuses
Iran's Fars news agency pushed back sharply, calling his characterisation incomplete and inconsistent with reality
The conditional ceasefire is in place, yet the strait remains effectively closed
More than a billion barrels of oil have been lost since the closure, with an additional hundred million barrels lost every week it stays shut
Good morning Sean, it's Monday, the twenty-fifth of May
Quick trivia: today is Towel Day — the annual tribute to Douglas Adams and The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Fans worldwide carry a towel today, because as any galactic hitchhiker knows, it's the most massively useful thing you can have
Eight-thirty kicks off with the MIP E2E Supply Chain Delivery Performance call on WebEx — note it runs into your nine o'clock bi-monthly catch-up with Venus on Teams, so you may need to juggle
Then the Weekly Planning and Look-Ahead with Bob and Rehan from ten to eleven, followed by the Potash L2 Fortnightly MOP
A brief team movement discussion at one-thirty, and the afternoon finishes with a family vaccination session from two-thirty to five at Osler Health in Vista Exchange
Archaeologists have decided to finally excavate a mystery metal box buried in Adam Park — a Second World War battlefield site under study for over a decade
What's inside could fill significant gaps in records from the Malayan campaign
Good evening Sean, it is Sunday evening, the twenty-fourth of May 2026
Quick fun fact: "Mayday" - the international distress call - has nothing to do with the month of May
It comes from the French phrase "M'aidez," meaning help me
Every time a pilot declares an emergency, they are essentially shouting French at air traffic control
Your Weekly Planning and Look-Ahead session with Bob and Rehan runs ten to eleven in the morning, then you have your thirty-minute catch-up with Pat at two in the afternoon
Asia Pacific markets closed higher today on growing optimism that the Iran conflict may be moving toward resolution, which cooled oil prices
Japan's Nikkei led the gains - up three and a half percent - after April exports surged fourteen point eight percent year-on-year, the fastest pace since January, driven largely by semiconductors
South Korea also confirmed it will launch twenty-four hour dollar-won spot trading from the sixth of July
